briefdescription.para: A standalone continuation byte (0x80-0xBF) without a valid start byte.
A two-byte sequence that encodes a value representable in one byte.
A two-byte sequence whose continuation byte is missing.
A two-byte sequence followed by a non-continuation byte.
A three-byte sequence that uses more bytes than required for the code-point.
A three-byte sequence with one or more missing continuation bytes.
A three-byte sequence containing an invalid continuation byte.
A UTF-8 sequence that decodes to a UTF-16 surrogate code-point.
A four-byte sequence that over-encodes a smaller code-point.
A four-byte sequence with one or more missing continuation bytes.
A four-byte sequence containing an invalid continuation byte.
A UTF-8 sequence that decodes to a code-point above U+10FFFF.
A byte that cannot start a valid UTF-8 sequence.
All supported malformed UTF-8 sequence categories for invalidUtf8().

memberdef.name: allUtf8Errors
memberdef.initializer: = std::array<Utf8Error, static_cast<std::size_t>(Utf8Error::_count)>{ Utf8Error::UnexpectedContinuationByte, Utf8Error::Overlong2ByteSequence, Utf8Error::Truncated2ByteSequence, Utf8Error::InvalidContinuationByteIn2ByteSequence, Utf8Error::Overlong3ByteSequence, Utf8Error::Truncated3ByteSequence, Utf8Error::InvalidContinuationByteIn3ByteSequence, Utf8Error::SurrogateCodePoint, Utf8Error::Overlong4ByteSequence, Utf8Error::Truncated4ByteSequence, Utf8Error::InvalidContinuationByteIn4ByteSequence, Utf8Error::CodePointBeyondUnicodeRange, Utf8Error::InvalidStartByte, }

detaileddescription.para: A list of all malformed UTF-8 categories supported by invalidUtf8().
This constant is convenient for parameterized tests that verify UTF-8 validation or error reporting against every malformed sequence generator provided by this API.

Get the code-point count for UTF-8 encoded text.
text
The UTF-8 encoded text to count the code-points for.
The number of code-points in the UTF-8 encoded text.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string_view text, const std::size_t maxLength) noexcept -> std::string
memberdef.name: toConsoleSafeString

detaileddescription.para: Create text that is safe for console output from UTF-8 encoded input.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

detaileddescription.para: Convert a string into a UTF-8 encoded std::string.
std::string_view and std::u8string_view are copied byte-for-byte. std::u16string_view, std::u32string_view and std::wstring_view are transcoded to UTF-8.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

detaileddescription.para: Convert a string into a UTF-32 encoded std::u32string.

std::u32string_view is copied unmodified. All other string views are decoded according to their encoding and converted to UTF-32 code-points. Invalid UTF-8 input is replaced with the Unicode replacement character (U+FFFD).

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

detaileddescription.para: Parse a hexadecimal byte string into a std::string.
Spaces are allowed only between complete byte groups, not inside them. Examples: "0102c8" or "01 02 A1".

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Parses two hexadecimal digits per element into a std::u8string.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Parses four hexadecimal digits per element into a std::u16string.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Parses eight hexadecimal digits per element into a std::u32string.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts. Parses one wchar_t value per element using sizeof(wchar_t) * 2 hexadecimal digits.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Utf8Error error, const std::optional< std::string_view > &prefix, const std::optional< std::string_view > &suffix) -> std::string
memberdef.name: invalidUtf8

detaileddescription.para: Generate a malformed UTF-8 byte sequence, optionally wrapped with valid prefix and suffix text.
This helper is intended for UTF-8 decoder and validator tests. The generated malformed sequence is inserted between the optional prefix and suffix without modifying those surrounding bytes.

parameterdescription.para: The malformed UTF-8 error kind to generate.
Optional valid UTF-8 bytes to prepend.
Optional valid UTF-8 bytes to append.

simplesect.para: A string containing the malformed UTF-8 sequence.

detaileddescription.para: Split a string into individual lines without copying the line contents.

This function returns string views into the original input and therefore does not allocate storage for the individual line contents. The original text must stay alive and unchanged while the returned views are used. Lines are split at newline characters (\n), and the resulting views do not include the newline characters. A trailing newline does not produce an additional empty line at the end.

parameterdescription.para: The text to split into lines.

simplesect.para: A vector of string views, each representing a line from the input text.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

detaileddescription.para: Split a string into individual lines and return owned strings for each line.
Lines are split at newline characters (\n), and the resulting strings do not include the newline characters. A trailing newline does not produce an additional empty line at the end.

parameterdescription.para: The text to split into lines.

simplesect.para: A vector containing one string per line from the input text.

briefdescription.para: This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.

detaileddescription.para: Test if the given string contains valid UTF-8 text.

This helper validates both the byte-level UTF-8 encoding and the decoded Unicode scalar values. Inputs containing malformed byte sequences, surrogate code-points, or code-points outside the Unicode range fail the current test with a diagnostic that includes the offending byte position.

This is an overloaded member function, provided for convenience. It differs from the above function only in what argument(s) it accepts.
test
The active test instance.
text
The UTF-8 text to validate.
Compare a container with lines, against a container with line patterns.

Common usage: void testLines() { // ... std::vector<std::string> actual = //... from test auto expected = std::vector<std::string>{ "the first line", "a second line"}; REQUIRE_EQUAL_LINES(actual, expected); }

Each line on the expected side can contain one optional asterisk (*) character, that matches any number of characters at this location. The expected line can also contain any number of question mark (?) characters that match exactly one character at this location.
If the number of lines differs, or if at least one line does not match, the test fails and reports a side-by-side comparison of the actual and expected lines.

parameterdescription.para: A range type that provides size(), begin(), end(), and string-like line values.
A range type that provides size(), begin(), end(), and pattern line values.
The produced lines from the test.
The expected lines or line patterns.

detaileddescription.para: Namespace for text helpers.

These text helper functions are mainly provided for handling diagnostic output and validating text in unit tests. They are designed to be robust but can be inefficient, as they internally convert the text to UTF-8 for processing, which can be costly for large strings.
For performance critical hot-paths in your tests, please use a custom implementation that avoids the UTF-8 conversion.
